description_long: This object is a long, double-edged sword with a straight blade and a pronounced central ridge running the length of the blade. The hilt features a crossguard that extends horizontally, providing hand protection, and a cylindrical handle that appears to be wrapped for grip. The pommel at the end of the handle is rounded and slightly bulbous, likely for balance and secure handling. The entire sword has a uniform dark metallic finish, giving it a sleek and formidable appearance. There are no visible engravings or decorative elements, emphasizing a utilitarian design. The proportions and style suggest it is modeled after a European longsword, suitable for two-handed use.
description: A long, double-edged, dark metallic sword with a straight blade, crossguard, and wrapped cylindrical handle. The design is sleek and functional, with no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight, double-edged blade, crossguard, and cylindrical handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the blade and the rounded pommel at the end of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and handle from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the blade and the handle's cylindrical shape.
